description: Envelope-spoof shape seen through SPF: From shows example.com but the SPF-authenticated envelope-from (smtp.mailfrom) is attacker-controlled evil.test. No Return-Path is present, so the rule is isolated to the smtp.mailfrom-vs-From comparison. smtpMailfromDomainMatchesFromDomain is false and a single low-severity smtpMailfrom.domainMismatch signal is emitted carrying the From domain, all smtp.mailfrom domains, and the mismatching subset. Low severity reflects that forwarders and ESPs also legitimately send under a different envelope-from.

authentication-results: mx.example.net; spf=pass smtp.mailfrom=evil.test; dmarc=pass header.from=example.com; dkim=pass header.d=example.com

authenticationResults.raw: mx.example.net; spf=pass smtp.mailfrom=evil.test; dmarc=pass header.from=example.com; dkim=pass header.d=example.com

signals.message: SPF smtp.mailfrom domain differs from the From domain.
